source_paper_title: Invariant States of Hydrodynamic Limits of Randomized Load-Balancing Networks
context:
Page 3, Section 1.1 (Introduction: Background and Motivation)

"Since the N-server stationary distributions are known to be tight (due to the results in [5]), to provide a rigorous proof of this convergence it would suffice to show that the solution to the hydrodynamic equations converges to the invariant state characterized here for a large class of initial conditions. Whereas this is a non-trivial problem that is relegated to future work, it is encouraging that analogous results have been recently established for a related problem."
title: Convergence of hydrodynamic solutions to the invariant state (and hence convergence of $N$-server steady states)
latex:
Consider the supermarket model (SQ\((d)\)) with fixed \(d\ge 2\) and arrival rate \(\lambda\in(0,1)\). Let \(G\) be a service-time distribution on \([0,\infty)\) with mean \(1\), density \(g\), survival function \( ar G(x)=1-G(x)\), and hazard rate \(h(x)=g(x)/ ar G(x)\) on \([0,L)\) where \(L:=\sup\{x\ge 0: ar G(x)>0\}\in(0,\infty]\). For \(\ell\in\mathbb N\), let \(
u_\ell(t)\) be a finite measure on \([0,L)\) representing the hydrodynamic-limit state, and define \(s_\ell(t):=\langle 1,
u_\ell(t)angle\). The (deterministic) hydrodynamic equations are: for all \(\ell\in\mathbb N\) and \(t\ge 0\),
\[
D_\ell(t):=\int_0^t \langle h,
u_\ell(s)angle\,ds,
\]
\[
\eta_1(t):=\lambda igl(1-s_1(t)^d igr)\,\delta_0,\qquad
\eta_\ell(t):=\lambda P_d igl(s_{\ell-1}(t),s_\ell(t) igr)\, igl(
u_{\ell-1}(t)-
u_\ell(t) igr),\ \ell\ge 2,
\]
where \(P_d(x,y)=(x^d-y^d)/(x-y)\) for \(x
e y\) and \(P_d(x,x)=d x^{d-1}\). The measures \(
u_\ell(t)\) satisfy, for every bounded continuous \(f\) and every \(\ell\in\mathbb N\),
\[
\langle f,
u_\ell(t)angle = \left\langle f(\cdot+t) rac{ ar G(\cdot+t)}{ ar G(\cdot)},
u_\ell(0)ightangle + \int_{[0,t]} f(t-s) ar G(t-s)\,dD_{\ell+1}(s)
+\int_0^t \left\langle f(\cdot+t-s) rac{ ar G(\cdot+t-s)}{ ar G(\cdot)},\eta_\ell(s)ightangle ds.
\]
Let \(
u^*=(
u^*_\ell)_{\ell\in\mathbb N}\) be the (physical) invariant state characterized in the paper (in particular satisfying \(\lim_{\ell	o\infty}\langle 1,
u^*_\ellangle=0\)).

Problem: Identify a broad class of service distributions \(G\) and initial conditions \(
u(0)\) for which the unique solution \(
u(t)\) to the hydrodynamic equations converges to \(
u^*\) as \(t	o\infty\) (e.g. in the product topology on \(\mathbb N\)-indexed measures, or equivalently coordinatewise weak convergence of \(
u_\ell(t)	o
u^*_\ell\) for every \(\ell\)). Establishing this global-attractor property would in turn yield, via tightness of the \(N\)-server stationary distributions, that the stationary distributions of the \(N\)-server SQ\((d)\) models converge to \(
u^*\) as \(N	o\infty\).
open_problem_quote: "Since the N-server stationary distributions are known to be tight (due to the results in [5]), to provide a rigorous proof of this convergence it would suffice to show that the solution to the hydrodynamic equations converges to the invariant state characterized here for a large class of initial conditions. Whereas this is a non-trivial problem that is relegated to future work, it is encouraging that analogous results have been recently established for a related problem."
